name: Voltron2
description: Improved Voltron with enhanced sensitivity, retaining fast kinetics and photostability.

spectrum.name: Voltron2 (JF525)
spectrum.note: Voltron2 is the Ace2 rhodopsin–HaloTag scaffold conjugated with the JF525-HaloTag ligand (canonical Voltron2525). The Neuron 2023 paper does not report excitation/emission spectra, so this is the digitized FPbase spectrum of the Janelia Fluor JF525-HaloTag conjugate (FPbase, exMax 534 nm, emMax 553–554 nm), shared with the JF525-conjugated Voltron and Positron sensors. The HaloTag conjugate is red-shifted ~9 nm versus free JF525 (~525/549 nm), which is why peakEx/peakEm are 534/554 rather than the free-dye values. Custom 1-nm-resolution arrays peak at 534 nm (ex) and 553 nm (em).

kinetics.note: Voltron2525 onset and decay kinetics were each best fit with a double exponential (Fig. 2D/2E, cultured neurons, +100 mV step from −70 mV, imaged at 3.2 kHz; all experiments at room temperature, 23°C per Methods). Stored values are amplitude-weighted mean τ, not the fast component alone. Onset: τfast 0.67 ms + τslow 3.26 ms with %fast ≈ 79% (Fig. 2E) → τ̄ = 0.79×0.67 + 0.21×3.26 = 1.21 ms. Decay: τfast 0.89 ms + τslow 6.27 ms with %fast ≈ 44% (Fig. 2E) → τ̄ = 0.44×0.89 + 0.56×6.27 = 3.90 ms. The text lists each pair as 'Voltron2525 vs Voltron525, respectively', so Voltron2525's values are the first of each pair (slow decay 6.27 ms, not the 4.74 ms that belongs to Voltron525).

Voltron2525, amplitude-weighted mean τ from the bi-exponential fit in Table S2 of the HVI+ paper (HEK293T whole-cell voltage clamp, room temperature). Activation (depolarizing step): τfast 2.51 ms (94.0%) + τslow 26.75 ms (6.0%) → τ̄ = 3.96 ms. Deactivation (hyperpolarizing step): τfast 3.85 ms (82.6%) + τslow 13.48 ms (17.4%) → τ̄ = 5.53 ms. Slower and with a larger fast-component τ than the origin-paper fit (1.21/3.90 ms): this measurement is in HEK293T sampled at only 1058 Hz (0.95 ms/frame), which cannot resolve Voltron2's sub-millisecond fast onset (0.67 ms in the origin paper), so the fit lumps it into a ~2.5 ms component; the origin paper measured cultured neurons at 3.2 kHz, both at room temperature (23°C).

Voltron2585 (JF585): single response half-time τ1/2 = 2.1 ± 0.1 ms in the depolarizing step (n = 5 cells, 1058 Hz, room temperature, HEK293T), converted to τ = t½/ln2 = 3.03 ms; separate on/off not resolved. Measured in the Solaris paper as a comparator to Solaris585.

dynamicRangeData.note: Voltron2525 ΔF/F0 for the standard −70→+30 mV (100 mV) voltage step, read off the F-V curve in Fig. 2B (cultured neurons, whole-cell voltage clamp, 1P, JF525). The −70→+30 mV step is the canonical 100-mV window used in the paper (Fig. 2B inset shows this step). The full F-V sweep extends to +50 mV where ΔF/F0 reaches ≈ −37 to −38%; the previously stored −38 corresponded to that +50 mV end of the −110→+50 mV sweep (a 120 mV span from −70), not the 100-mV step, so it has been corrected to the −70→+30 mV value.

Voltron2525 (JF525): −27.5 ± 2.1% ΔF/F0 per 100 mV in HEK293T cells under whole-cell voltage clamp, measured in the Vega paper (1P wide-field, 525 nm excitation per Table S3); Fig. 1C compares Vega, Ace-mNeon2, JEDI-1P, and Voltron2525.
Voltron2525: −21.2 ± 1.3% ΔF/F0 per 100 mV (−70 to +30 mV, n = 5) in HEK293T cells under whole-cell voltage clamp at 1058 Hz, from Table S2 of the HVI+ paper.
Voltron2585 (JF585): −22% ΔF/F0 per 100 mV in HEK293T cells, measured in the Solaris paper (Fig. 1C, black curve) as a comparator to Solaris585 (−53%).

ΔF/F read from the Voltron2 (gray) F-V trace between -70 mV (baseline 0) and +30 mV in the Janelia patch-assay F-V curve figure (1P widefield, -20 mV steps, n=7); full sweep -110 to +50 mV spans ~+21% to -33.5%.

sensitivityData.note: Voltron2525: −12.0 ± 1.5% ΔF/F0 per AP (n = 5; magnitude stored, negative-going) in cultured rat hippocampal neurons at 484 Hz, 1P epifluorescence (532 nm), from Table S2 of the HVI+ paper.

Median single-AP |ΔF/F0| read from the Voltron2 (gray) boxplot in the Janelia patch-assay spike-detection figure (1P widefield, PatcherBot cultured neurons, n≈9); negative-going, median ≈ -15% (magnitude stored).

Voltron2585 (JF585): −12.3 ± 0.7% ΔF/F0 per AP (n = 7 cells; magnitude stored, negative-going) in cultured rat hippocampal neurons, 1P widefield 594 nm, imaged at 484 Hz. Measured in the Solaris paper under conditions matched to Solaris585 (−28.1%), whose primary subject is Solaris.

subthresholdData.note: Voltron2525 subthreshold slope 0.54%/mV ± 0.01%/mV in the −90 to −50 mV range (cultured neurons, n = 8 cells), an almost 3-fold higher slope than Voltron525 (0.21%/mV). Reported in the running main text; not a labeled figure panel (Fig. 3D is the synPSP sensitivity index d'/mV of Voltron2585, a different dye/metric).

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, uM Workstation whole-cell current clamp, single evoked AP (2 nA, 2 ms pulse)
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM ≈ 4.0 ms, DERIVED by digitizing the Voltron2525 (red) ΔF/F-vs-time fluorescence waveform in Fig. 2F (the bottom-right optical trace; representative single evoked AP, current clamp) — Voltron2525 is the origin paper's primary subject. Time axis 0–100 ms (2.59 px/ms); baseline from the flat pre-spike segment, peak = max negative deflection (≈ −7.6% here, followed by a positive post-spike overshoot), FWHM = the span where the trace exceeds the half-baseline-to-peak level (HSV-traced, overlay-verified). Imaged on the uM Workstation at its standard 985 Hz frame rate (Methods; the separate Fig. 2D kinetics panel used 3.2 kHz). This OPTICAL FWHM convolves the sensor's ~1–4 ms response kinetics and is much broader than the paper's ELECTRICAL patch-clamp AP half-width (1.6 ± 0.1 ms for Voltron2525). Representative single trace (n=1 shown), so the value is approximate; the Voltron2525 (4.0 ms) vs Voltron525 (5.8 ms) optical-FWHM difference reflects single-trace variability rather than a robust kinetic difference (the paper reports similar kinetics and near-identical electrical AP half-widths, 1.6 vs 1.5 ms).

Optical single-AP FWHM ≈ 7 ms for Voltron2585 (JF585), DERIVED by digitizing the rightmost (dark-red, −12.3 ± 0.7%, n=7) averaged optical waveform in Fig. 2B of the Solaris paper, where Voltron2585 was co-plotted as a comparator to the Solaris dye series under matched conditions (cultured rat hippocampal neurons, current-injected single APs, 1P widefield 594 nm, 484 Hz camera frame rate). Calibration: the panel's 20 ms time scale bar spans ≈32 px at 400 dpi (1.6 px/ms), and each optical sub-panel is ≈270 px ≈ 169 ms wide; the dark-red spike half-max span is ≈11–12 px → ≈7 ms. The paper reports NO temperature for the Fig. 2B neuron recordings (Methods say only 'room temperature'), so temperature is 'room temperature', not a specific value. This OPTICAL FWHM is broader than the paper's HEK293T voltage-clamp step half-time (τ½ = 2.1 ± 0.1 ms, Fig. 1, a DIFFERENT experiment) — that τ½ does NOT corroborate this optical AP FWHM and should not be conflated. Reliability is limited: at 484 Hz (2.07 ms/frame) only ~3–5 samples span this sharp spike, so ±2 ms uncertainty. Measured in the Solaris paper (Yang et al. 2024), not the origin Neuron 2023 paper. (History: a prior sweep stored 2.2 ms using an ~8×-too-large scale-bar calibration; independent re-digitization at the verified 1.6 px/ms calibration gives ~7 ms.)

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cerebellar granular-layer Golgi/glycinergic interneurons, in vivo, awake, Voltron2-ST552 (JF552)
apWidthData.note: Optical spike FWHM ≈ 0.85 ms (red bar, n = 11) for Voltron2-ST552 (JF552-HaloTag), read off the 'FWHM (ms)' bar panel in Fig. S9d of the 2Photron paper (Villette et al. 2024), where Voltron2-ST552 was cross-measured as a comparator to 2Photron-ST552 and JEDI-2P-Kv. Two-photon ULoVE point-scanning at >3.5 kHz in awake-mouse cerebellar granular-layer interneurons in vivo. Sub-millisecond — below the 1–20 ms optical-FWHM range typical of 1P widefield traces — because the ultrafast >3.5 kHz 2P sampling resolves the true membrane spike far better than 484–985 Hz 1P imaging (see entries above). Cross-measured in 2Photron's (2photron) paper, not the origin Neuron 2023 paper.

photostabilityData.note: Voltron2525 photobleached 41% ± 1% over 10 min in cultured neurons (→ 59% remaining; Fig. 2I, room temperature). Illumination intensity is the measured imaging power for the JF525-labeled sensor in this characterization assay, 14.7 mW/mm² in the imaging plane (510/25 ex, 545/40 em, 525 dichroic; Methods). The previously stored 80 mW/mm² is not stated anywhere in the paper and has been corrected.
Voltron2525 photobleaching half-life of 180 ± 32 s in HEK293T cells under continuous 532 nm illumination at 5.0 W/cm² (= 50 mW/mm²); 50% remaining at 3.0 min (Table S5).

photobleach.note: Origin-paper photobleaching curve for Voltron2525 (Voltron2 + JF525) in cultured neurons over 10 min at room temperature (23°C), digitized from the red curve in Fig. 2I (blue = Voltron525 co-plotted). Continuous 1P widefield imaging at 985 Hz through the JF525 filter cube (510/25 ex, 545/40 em, 525 dichroic) with a measured power of 14.7 mW/mm² in the imaging plane (Methods). Ends at F = 0.59 at 600 s, matching the paper's stated 41% ± 1% reduction over 10 min (→ 59% remaining). Fit to a stretched exponential F = exp(−(t/1156.3)^0.939), t₇₅% ≈ 307 s (well inside the 600 s window; interp of the digitized points gives 306 s). Companion curve: Voltron525 in the same figure.

Photobleaching curve for Voltron2525 (Voltron2 + JF525) in HEK293T cells under continuous 1P 532 nm illumination at 5.0 W/cm² (= 50 mW/mm²), digitized from the yellow curve in Fig. S7 (hue-separated from the amber Positron525 curve; curve-tracked to skip the same-colored label). Fit to a stretched exponential F = exp(−(t/270.3)^1.053), t₇₅% ≈ 83 s (inside the ~315 s window). Fit t½ = 191 s matches the cohort half-life 180 ± 32 s (Table S5, n = 18). Companion to HVI+-Cy3b / HVI-Cy3b / Positron525 in the same figure.
